title: Queues, Streams, Super Streams, Retention, and Replay · Compiler Case: Superstream Partitioning
summary: A high-throughput replay workload uses one quorum queue with a ten-million-message backlog, while another service selects streams but expects destructive consumption and ordinary queue client locality. Planned artifact: Add and verify the superstream partitioning increment in queue-versus-stream workload decision and replay plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use super streams to partition throughput by key while accounting for partitions, routing, per-partition order, skew, and consumer coordination.
Define age and byte retention, segment behavior, start offset, consumer progress, replay authorization, and late-reader expectations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Superstream Partitioning · Theory Superstream Partitioning
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 2547 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to choose queue, stream, or super stream from replay, backlog, throughput, and order-key evidence; allow exactly one terminal acknowledgement decision owner; then remove the dead-letter binding and expose the resulting retention risk. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Superstream Partitioning contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Superstream Partitioning · Theory Superstream Partitioning.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use super streams to partition throughput by key while accounting for partitions, routing, per-partition order, skew, and consumer coordination. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Superstream Partitioning · Theory Superstream Partitioning, keep Superstream Partitioning connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.
